sheet_name: แปลนหลังคา
view_title: แปลนหลังคา
source_image: image/บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_02_หน้า07.png
grid_source: raw_json_ตัวที่ใช้งานจริง/02บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_02_หน้า00_gridline.json

element.grid_ref: F-A x 1-4, plus overhangs; rectangular notch at south end (matching จอดรถ/carport open area, B-A x 1-2, no roof over it)
element.description: หลังคาเหล็กรีดลอน (corrugated metal sheet roofing), hatched vertically over the whole roofed plan; two 'SLOPE 6°' labels printed vertically down the two roof faces (one each side of the central ridge/valley line running north-south near grid3) indicating fall direction/pitch of approximately 6 degrees.
element.ridge_or_valley_lines: one internal fold line runs roughly north-south near grid3 (between the two 'SLOPE 6°' labels), consistent with a low-slope roof that steps or folds rather than a single continuous monopitch — not confidently classified as a true ridge vs. valley at this resolution

element.grid_ref: beyond A x 3-4 (over จอดรถ carport), level +3.65
element.description: หลังคา ค.ส.ล. (concrete canopy/roof slab) extending south over the carport beyond the main roofed footprint, at a slightly lower level (+3.65) than the two side canopies (+3.75); dimension 3.20 wide printed at the bottom

warnings: Unlike house 1 (where floor plan and roof plan share one sheet, A-04), this house prints the roof plan on its own dedicated sheet A-05 — no split needed, this file covers the whole sheet.
The 'SLOPE 6°' labels are printed vertically along both roof faces, same convention and same 6-degree value as house 1's roof plan — read at medium-high confidence given the small stylized text.

Roof plan shows a rectangular notch (no roof) over the open จอดรถ carport area (B-A x 3-4), consistent with the floor plan showing จอดรถ as an open, uncovered carport apart from the separate concrete canopy extension noted above.

Actual roof framing (purlins/rafters/trusses) is not detailed on this sheet — references 'โครงหลังคา ดูแบบวิศวกรรมโครงสร้าง' (structural engineering drawings, outside this extraction's architecture-only page range 03-21).

CORRECTED_2026-07-16: the earlier blanket conclusion here ('no dummy grid line required for any of them since they are pure roof/canopy overhangs') is superseded. The metal-roof eave overhangs themselves (1.30m east/west, 1.50m north) remain pure roof dimensions with no dummy grid of their own — but the +3.75 concrete side canopies within them ARE now pinned to the master's dummy grids 1'(-1.0)/4'(10.6), since the floor plan shows real built floor area (ห้องน้ำ1/ห้องน้ำ2) extending to those lines. Geometry is consistent: the 1.30m roof eave = 1.00m wall/canopy extension (to 1'/4') + 0.30m of roof projecting past it.
